787	% defined, and suppose that V∖U has codimension 1. Then there is a prime divisor Z in V∖U.

789	% fractions k(V). The map φ defines a morphism of schemes Spec(k(V)) → W, which the valuative
790	% criterion of properness (Hartshorne 1977, II 4.7) shows extends to a morphism Spec(O_Z) → W.
791	% This implies that φ has a representative defined on an open subset that meets Z in a nonempty
792	% set, which is a contradiction." [Milne, Thm 3.1, \S I.3, doc p.~17.]
793	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF (Lemma 3.3, the difference-map): "Define a rational map Φ:V×V ⇢ G,
794	% (x,y) ↦ φ(x)·φ(y)⁻¹. ... Thus φ is defined at x if and only if Φ is defined at (x,x). ...
795	% Suppose φ is not defined at x. Then for some f ∈ Im(Φ*), (x,x) ∈ div(f)₁, and clearly Φ is
796	% not defined at the points (y,y) ∈ Δ ∩ div(f)₁. This is a subset of pure codimension one in Δ,
797	% and when we identify it with a subset of V, it is a subset of V of codimension one passing
798	% through x on which φ is not defined." [Milne, Lemma 3.3, \S I.3, doc p.~17--18.]

800	  \textit{Source: Milne, Abelian Varieties, Theorem~3.2.}
801	  The statement is Theorem~3.1 combined with Lemma~3.3. By Theorem~3.1 (a consequence of the
802	  valuative criterion of properness, Mathlib \texttt{IsProper}/\texttt{ValuativeCriterion},
803	  applied at the discrete valuation ring of each codimension-\(1\) point), a rational map from the
804	  normal \(V\) to the complete \(A\) is defined outside a closed set of codimension \(\geq 2\). By
805	  Lemma~3.3, the indeterminacy locus of a rational map into a \emph{group} variety is either
806	  empty or pure of codimension \(1\): forming the difference map
807	  \(\Phi(x, y) = \varphi(x)\,\varphi(y)^{-1} \colon V \times V \dashrightarrow A\), one shows
808	  \(\varphi\) is defined at \(x\) iff \(\Phi\) is defined at the diagonal point \((x, x)\), and an
809	  indeterminacy point produces a pure-codimension-\(1\) component of the non-domain. The two
810	  facts are compatible only if the indeterminacy locus is empty; hence \(\alpha\) is everywhere
811	  defined.
812	\end{proof}
813	\begin{remark}
814	  \label{rmk:thm32_codim1_mathlib_gap}
815	  \textbf{Formalisation risk: the pure-codimension-\(1\) step (Lemma 3.3) has no Mathlib
816	  Weil-divisor support.} Theorem~3.1's everywhere-extension-outside-codimension-\(2\) is within
817	  reach of Mathlib's valuative criterion of properness
818	  (\texttt{ValuativeCriterion}/\texttt{IsProper.of\_valuativeCriterion}), applied at the
819	  discrete valuation ring \(\mathcal O_{V, Z}\) of each codimension-\(1\) point \(Z\). But Lemma~3.3,
820	  which rules out codimension-\(1\) indeterminacy for maps into a group variety, is proved in
821	  Milne via Weil-divisor theory (\(\mathrm{div}(f)_0 - \mathrm{div}(f)_1\), prime divisors,
822	  tangent cones) for which Mathlib currently has \emph{no} usable API at this generality. This
823	  is the route's riskiest sub-build. An open question for the prover: whether a \emph{pointwise}
824	  valuative extension --- showing, at each codimension-\(1\) point \(Z\) separately, that the
825	  morphism \(\Spec k(V) \to A\) extends over \(\Spec \mathcal O_{V, Z}\) using only that \(A\) is
826	  proper and a group (so the difference-map argument can be run \emph{locally} at \(Z\) without
827	  globally building \(\mathrm{div}(f)\)) --- side-steps building divisor theory entirely.
828	  \textbf{This surface extension is \emph{not} on the genus-\(0\) critical path (corrected, iter-164).}
829	  An earlier draft routed the genus-\(0\) base case through this extension, by collapsing the
830	  additive-defect map \(\psi(x, y) = f(x + y) - f(x) - f(y)\) on the \emph{affine} surface
831	  \(\mathbb G_a \times \mathbb G_a\) and then extending \(\psi\) over the complete surface
832	  \(\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb P^1\) --- which genuinely \emph{would} need Theorem~3.2's surface case
833	  (Milne Theorem~3.4 / Lemma~3.3). That detour is unnecessary. The 4th route
834	  (\cref{rmk:base_case_fourth_route}) replaces the two-variable affine defect map by the
835	  \emph{total} translation action \(\sigma \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_a \to \mathbb P^1\)
836	  (\cref{def:gaTranslationP1}): \(h := \sigma \fatsemi f\) is a total morphism on $\mathbb P^1
837	  \times \mathbb G_a\( (no extension needed), and the complete-source factor is \)\mathbb P^1$
838	  itself, so the additive decomposition \cref{lem:hom_additivity_over_product} applies directly.
839	  Consequently \cref{lem:rational_map_to_av_extends} (Theorem~3.2), the codim-\(1\) indeterminacy
840	  sub-build (Lemma~3.3), and the Weil-divisor gap discussed above are all confined to
841	  \textbf{Route~A} (the positive-genus Albanese universal property, Milne \S III.6); they are
842	  retained in this chapter solely as Route-A inputs and may be deferred until Route~A consumes
843	  them. The genus-\(0\) close no longer depends on any of them.
844	\end{remark}

846	\section{The genus-\(0\) base objects and the \(\mathbb G_m\)-scaling action (the primary route)}
847	\begin{definition}
848	[Concrete genus-\(0\) base objects: \(\mathbb P^1\), \(\mathbb G_a\), \(\mathbb G_m\) over \(\bar k\)]
849	  \label{def:genus0_base_objects}
850	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveLineBar}
851	  % Archon-original Lean encoding of standard objects; the mathematics is textbook (Hartshorne
852	  % I.2--3 for the projective line; the group laws on \(\mathbb A^1\) are elementary). No external
853	  % % SOURCE QUOTE is needed for the Lean encoding itself.
854	  Work over \(\Spec \bar k\) with \(\bar k\) algebraically closed. The primary \(\mathbb G_m\)-scaling
855	  route (and its \(\mathbb G_a\)-additive alternative) needs only three concrete objects of
856	  \(\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec \bar k)\):
857	  \begin{itemize}
858	    \item \(\mathbb P^1 = \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\), the projective line --- a smooth proper
859	      geometrically irreducible curve of genus \(0\) (Mathlib: projective space, or \(\mathrm{Proj}\)
860	      of the graded polynomial ring; \lean name \texttt{ProjectiveLineBar}). It carries
861	      the distinguished \(\bar k\)-points \(0\), \(1\), \(\infty\) and the two standard affine charts
862	      \(\mathbb A^1 = \mathbb P^1 \setminus \{\infty\}\) (coordinate \(x\)) and
863	      \(\mathbb P^1 \setminus \{0\}\) (coordinate \(u = 1/x\)).
864	    \item \(\mathbb G_a = \mathbb A^1_{\bar k}\), the affine line as the \emph{additive} group object
865	      (Mathlib: \texttt{AffineSpace}\,\(\mathbb A(1; \Spec \bar k)\) with the \(\mathtt{GrpObj}\)
866	      additive structure \((x, y) \mapsto x + y\), identity \(0\); \lean name \texttt{Ga}).
867	      As a scheme \(\mathbb G_a = \mathbb A^1 = \mathbb P^1 \setminus \{\infty\}\), and the open
868	      immersion \(\mathbb G_a \hookrightarrow \mathbb P^1\) has dense (cofinite) image.
869	    \item \(\mathbb G_m = \mathbb A^1 \setminus \{0\} = \mathbb P^1 \setminus \{0, \infty\}\), the
870	      \emph{multiplicative} group object (group law \((x, y) \mapsto xy\), identity \(1\); \lean name
871	      \texttt{Gm}). Its open immersion into \(\mathbb P^1\) also has dense image.
872	  \end{itemize}
873	  All three are of finite type over \(\bar k\); \(\mathbb P^1\) is proper, while \(\mathbb G_a\) and
874	  \(\mathbb G_m\) are affine (hence not complete). These are the only ``genus-\(0\)'' geometric inputs
875	  the base case needs.
876	\end{definition}

957	\begin{lemma}
958	[\(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) is proper over \(\Spec \bar k\)]
959	  \label{lem:projectiveLineBar_isProper}
960	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.projectiveLineBar_isProper}
961	  \uses{def:genus0_base_objects}
962	  % Archon-original instance; the mathematics is textbook (Hartshorne
963	  % II.4 + the standard properness of Proj of a finitely generated graded
964	  % algebra over a Noetherian base; here the base is a field, hence trivially
965	  % Noetherian). No external SOURCE QUOTE needed.
966	  The structure morphism \((\mathtt{ProjectiveLineBar}\,\bar k).\mathrm{hom}
967	  \colon \mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to \Spec \bar k\) is proper.
968	  \medskip
969	  \noindent\textbf{Proof sketch.} Unfold \((\mathtt{ProjectiveLineBar}\,\bar k)
970	  .\mathrm{hom}\) as the composition
971	  \(\Proj.\mathrm{toSpecZero}\,(\mathrm{projectiveLineBarGrading}\,\bar k)
972	  \fatsemi \Spec.\mathrm{map}\,(\mathrm{algebraMap}\,\bar k\,
973	  \uparrow{\mathcal A_0})\). The first factor is proper by Mathlib's
974	  \(\mathtt{Proj.instIsProperToSpecZero}\): the polynomial algebra
975	  \(\bar k[X_0, X_1]\) is finite type over its degree-\(0\) piece
976	  \(\mathcal A_0\), and the \(\mathtt{IsScalarTower}\) +
977	  \(\mathtt{Algebra.FiniteType}\) chain (via
978	  \(\mathtt{Algebra.FiniteType.of\_restrictScalars\_finiteType}\)) supplies the
979	  finite-type witness. The second factor \(\Spec.\mathrm{map}\,(\mathrm{algebraMap}
980	  \,\bar k\,\uparrow{\mathcal A_0})\) is an isomorphism: the algebra map
981	  \(\bar k \to \uparrow{\mathcal A_0}\) is bijective because \(\mathcal A_0\)
982	  is the constants subalgebra of \(\bar k[X_0, X_1]\) (injectivity via
983	  \(\mathtt{MvPolynomial.C\_injective}\); surjectivity via
984	  \(\mathtt{MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent\_of\_mem}\) +
985	  \(\mathtt{homogeneousComponent\_zero}\)). Composition of proper with iso is
986	  proper. LOC: \(\sim 40\) (already landed axiom-clean).
987	\end{lemma}

1088	\begin{lemma}
1089	[\(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) is geometrically irreducible]
1090	  \label{lem:projectiveLineBar_geomIrred}
1091	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.projectiveLineBar_geomIrred}
1092	  \uses{def:genus0_base_objects}
1093	  % Archon-original instance; the mathematics is textbook (Hartshorne
1094	  % II.4 + I.1; \(\Proj\) of an integral domain over a field is
1095	  % integral and geometrically integral when the field is algebraically
1096	  % closed and the polynomial ring is well-defined). No external SOURCE
1097	  % QUOTE needed.
1098	  The structure morphism \((\mathtt{ProjectiveLineBar}\,\bar k)
1099	  .\mathrm{hom} \colon \mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to \Spec \bar k\) is
1100	  geometrically irreducible: for every field extension \(K/\bar k\),
1101	  the base change \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \times_{\Spec \bar k} \Spec K
1102	  = \mathbb P^1_K\) is irreducible. Since \(\bar k\) is algebraically
1103	  closed and \(\bar k[X_0, X_1]\) is the polynomial ring (integral
1104	  domain), \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k} = \Proj(\bar k[X_0, X_1])\) is
1105	  integral, and the base change to any field extension \(K\) preserves
1106	  integrality (the homogeneous coordinate ring \(K[X_0, X_1]\) is
1107	  itself an integral domain, hence \(\mathbb P^1_K\) is integral and
1108	  irreducible).
1109	\end{lemma}
1110	\begin{proof}
1111	  \uses{def:genus0_base_objects}
1112	  % NOTE (iter-197 blueprint-writer): the iter-196 lean-vs-blueprint-checker
1113	  % flagged this proof as under-specified for Lean closure. The expanded sketch
1114	  % below decomposes the argument into five named sub-helpers (A--E), pinning
1115	  % the load-bearing Proj-base-change iso (Helper A) to a future Lean target;
1116	  % until Helper A lands, the public `projectiveLineBar_geomIrred` instance
1117	  % remains a Tier-3 named scaffold sorry.
1118	  We decompose the argument into five sub-helpers. The load-bearing piece is
1119	  Helper~A (the Proj base-change isomorphism), which is currently a Mathlib gap
1120	  at the pinned commit \(\mathtt{b80f227}\) and must be supplied as a project-side
1121	  substrate (estimated 100--200 LOC). The remaining helpers are then either
1122	  Mathlib-direct or short combinatorial bridges. Total estimated closure cost
1123	  across the chain: 200--350 LOC over multiple iters.
1124	  \medskip
1125	  \noindent\textbf{Sub-helper~A (load-bearing): base-change of \(\Proj\).}
1126	  \(\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.baseChangeIso}\) (future target;
1127	  Mathlib upstreaming candidate or project supplement). For a graded ring
1128	  \(\mathcal A \colon \mathbb N \to \sigma\) over a base ring \(R\) and an
1129	  \(R\)-algebra \(K\), there is a natural isomorphism of schemes over \(\Spec R\):
1130	  \[ \Proj\,(K \otimes_R \mathcal A)
1131	     \ \cong\ \Proj\,\mathcal A \,\times_{\Spec R}\, \Spec K. \]
1132	  This is the standard Stacks Project base-change formula for relative
1133	  \(\Proj\) (Stacks tag 0BLW, ``base change of relative Proj''). At the pinned
1134	  Mathlib commit \(\mathtt{b80f227}\) no version of this isomorphism is shipped
1135	  for the abstract graded-ring \(\Proj\) construction; the project-side build
1136	  needs to construct the iso functorially, prove naturality in \(K\), and
1137	  expose the \(\Spec K\)-projection compatibility for downstream consumers.
1138	  \medskip
1139	  \noindent\textbf{Sub-helper~B: chart-iso reparameterisation over an extension
1140	  field.} \(\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso.baseChange}\)
1141	  (future target). The chart-ring iso of \cref{def:proj_chart_ring_iso}
1142	  identifies \(\mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i \cong \bar k[u]\) over the base
1143	  field \(\bar k\). Helper~B re-runs the same construction over an arbitrary
1144	  field extension \(K / \bar k\) to obtain
1145	  \(K \otimes_{\bar k} \mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i \cong K[u]\) (the affine
1146	  chart of \(\mathbb P^1_K\)). This is a routine base-change of the iso of
1147	  \cref{def:proj_chart_ring_iso} and carries no new substrate gap once
1148	  Helper~A is in scope.
1149	  \medskip
1150	  \noindent\textbf{Sub-helper~C: the base-changed graded ring is a graded
1151	  integral domain.} For \(K\) any field extension of \(\bar k\), the tensor
1152	  product \(K \otimes_{\bar k} \bar k[X_0, X_1]\) is a graded \(K\)-algebra
1153	  canonically identified with the polynomial ring \(K[X_0, X_1]\), which is
1154	  itself an integral domain. Consequently the underlying scheme of
1155	  \(\Proj\,(K \otimes_{\bar k} \bar k[X_0, X_1])\) is integral on every basic
1156	  open \(D_+(X_i)\) (each chart ring is a localisation of the integral domain
1157	  \(K[X_0, X_1]\), hence a domain), and a pointwise check at generic points
1158	  gives integrality of the whole scheme. (Currently un-pinned; iter-197+ prover
1159	  names the Lean target as it materialises the helper.)
1160	  \medskip
1161	  \noindent\textbf{Sub-helper~D: \(\Proj\) of an integral graded domain over a
1162	  field is integral.} Mathlib's
1163	  \(\mathtt{Proj.instIsIntegral}\)-style instance (when the irrelevant-ideal
1164	  cofinality assumption holds, as it does for the standard polynomial grading)
1165	  packages the global integrality of \(\Proj\,(K[X_0, X_1])\). Integrality
1166	  implies irreducibility of the underlying scheme. (Currently un-pinned;
1167	  iter-197+ prover names the Lean target as it materialises the helper.)
1168	  \medskip
1169	  \noindent\textbf{Sub-helper~E: assemble via Helper~A.} For every field
1170	  extension \(K / \bar k\), Helper~A gives
1171	  \((\mathtt{ProjectiveLineBar}\,\bar k) \times_{\Spec \bar k} \Spec K
1172	  \cong \Proj\,(K \otimes_{\bar k} \bar k[X_0, X_1]) = \mathbb P^1_K\); Helper~C
1173	  + Helper~D give that \(\mathbb P^1_K\) is integral, hence irreducible.
1174	  Combined with the algebraic-closure hypothesis on \(\bar k\) (which makes the
1175	  generic-point check uniform across all extensions), this gives
1176	  \(\mathtt{GeometricallyIrreducible}\,(\mathtt{ProjectiveLineBar}\,\bar k).\mathrm{hom}\).
1177	  (Currently un-pinned; iter-197+ prover names the Lean target as it

2671	\begin{proof}
2672	  \textit{Source: Milne, Abelian Varieties, Proposition~3.9.}
2673	  Write \(A\) additively (an abelian variety is commutative). We give the conceptual
2674	  \emph{completeness} proof as the abstract argument, then record Milne's elementary $\mathbb
2675	  G_a/\mathbb G_m\( incompatibility, which applies whenever \)\varphi$ extends to a morphism on
2676	  \(\mathbb P^1\) (as it does in the genus-\(0\) application).
2677	  \textbf{Completeness proof.} The image \(\varphi(\mathbb G_a)\) is a connected subgroup of \(A\)
2678	  (image of the connected \(\mathbb G_a\) under a homomorphism). As the image of a homomorphism of
2679	  algebraic groups it is a \emph{closed} subgroup of \(A\), hence \emph{complete} (closed in the
2680	  complete \(A\)); as a homomorphic image of the \emph{affine} \(\mathbb G_a\) it is moreover
2681	  \emph{affine}. A connected variety that is simultaneously complete and affine over \(\bar k\) is a
2682	  single point: its ring of global regular functions is a field finite over \(\bar k\), hence equal
2683	  to \(\bar k\) (the \(\Gamma = \bar k\) collapse already packaged for proper integral sources in
2684	  \cref{lem:eq_comp_of_isAffine_of_properIntegral}). Therefore \(\varphi(\mathbb G_a)\) is one point;
2685	  being a homomorphism, \(\varphi\) sends \(0\) there, so the point is \(0_A\) and
2686	  \(\varphi = \mathtt{toUnit}\,\mathbb G_a \fatsemi \eta_A\).
2687	  \medskip
2688	  \noindent\textbf{Milne's elementary \(\mathbb G_a/\mathbb G_m\) incompatibility (when \(\varphi\)
2689	  extends to \(\mathbb P^1\)).} Suppose \(\varphi = f|_{\mathbb G_a}\) for a morphism
2690	  \(f \colon \mathbb P^1 \to A\) (the genus-\(0\) situation). Set \(g := f - f(1)\), so \(g(1) = 0_A\), and
2691	  form \(h_\times := \sigma_\times \fatsemi g \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \to A\) with the
2692	  scaling companion \(\sigma_\times\) of \cref{def:gaTranslationP1}. Applying the additive
2693	  decomposition \cref{lem:hom_additivity_over_product} with \(V = \mathbb P^1\) (complete),
2694	  \(W = \mathbb G_m\), base points \(1 \in \mathbb P^1\) and \(1 \in \mathbb G_m\) (so
2695	  \(h_\times(1, 1) = g(1) = 0_A\)), the axis restrictions are \(x \mapsto g(x)\) and
2696	  \(\lambda \mapsto g(\lambda)\), whence \(g(\lambda x) = g(x) + g(\lambda)\) on \(\bar k^\times\);
2697	  re-expanding \(g\) gives the multiplicative relation
2698	  \(\varphi(xy) = \varphi(x) + \varphi(y) + c\) with \(c = -\varphi(1)\). Now \(\varphi\) is at once
2699	  additive, \(\varphi(x + y) = \varphi(x) + \varphi(y)\), and ``multiplicative-additive'',
2700	  \(\varphi(xy) = \varphi(x) + \varphi(y) + c\). Computing \(\varphi((x + x')\,y)\) in two ways for
2701	  \(x, x' \in \bar k\), \(y \in \bar k^\times\),
2702	  \[ \varphi((x+x')y) = \varphi(xy) + \varphi(x'y) = \varphi(x) + \varphi(x') + 2\varphi(y) + 2c, \]
2703	  while also $\varphi((x+x')y) = \varphi(x+x') + \varphi(y) + c = \varphi(x) + \varphi(x') +
2704	  \varphi(y) + c\(; equating gives \)\varphi(y) = -c = \varphi(1)\( for every \)y \in \bar k^\times$.
2705	  Hence \(\varphi\) is constant on the dense \(\mathbb G_m \subset \mathbb G_a\), so constant on
2706	  \(\mathbb G_a\) (density, \(A\) separated), with value \(\varphi(0) = 0_A\). This is Milne's argument
2707	  verbatim and uses only the proven \cref{lem:hom_additivity_over_product} plus the scaling action,
2708	  avoiding the ``image is a closed subgroup'' input of the completeness proof. \qedhere
2709	\end{proof}

2843	  Let \(\bar k\) be algebraically closed and \(A\) an abelian variety over \(\bar k\). Then every
2844	  morphism \(f \colon \mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to A\) is constant: \(f(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k})\) is a
2845	  single \(\bar k\)-point.
2846	\end{proposition}
2847	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "PROOF. We may suppose that \(k\) is algebraically closed. By assumption
2848	% there is a rational map \(\mathbb A^n \dashrightarrow V\) with dense image, and the composite
2849	% of this with \(\alpha\) extends to a morphism $\beta \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \cdots \times

2851	% regular maps \(\beta_i \colon \mathbb P^1 \to A\) such that $\beta(x_1, \ldots, x_d) = \sum
2852	% \beta_i(x_i)\(, and the lemma shows that each \)\beta_i$ is constant."
2853	\begin{proof}
2854	  This is the single-factor (\(d = 1\)) instance of Milne's Proposition~3.10, where the
2855	  unirational variety is \(\mathbb P^1\) itself. We give the project's \textbf{primary} proof, the
2856	  \emph{\(\mathbb G_m\)-scaling shortcut}: it proves constancy \emph{in one shot} from the proven
2857	  additive decomposition (\cref{lem:hom_additivity_over_product}) and a scaling fixed point,
2858	  without the intermediate ``\(f|_{\mathbb G_a}\) additive'' step and \emph{without} the
2859	  \(\mathrm{Hom}(\mathbb G_a, A) = 0\) triviality lemma. The theorem of the cube is \emph{not} used
2860	  (\cref{rmk:cube_not_needed}), and the map \(f\) is already a morphism out of the complete
2861	  nonsingular \(\mathbb P^1\), so Milne's Theorem~3.2 (\cref{lem:rational_map_to_av_extends}) is
2862	  \emph{not} invoked either.
2863	  \textbf{Reduction to a base point.} Choose the scaling fixed point $0 \in \mathbb G_a \subset
2864	  \mathbb P^1\( (one of the two fixed points of \)\sigma_\times\(). Translating in the group \)A$ by
2865	  \(-f(0)\), we may assume \(f(0) = 0_A\); un-translating at the end restores the original value.
2866	  \textbf{The scaling shortcut.} Form the \emph{total} morphism
2867	  \[ h := \sigma_\times \fatsemi f \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \to A, \qquad
2868	     \text{``}h(x, \lambda) = f(\lambda x)\text{''}, \]
2869	  using the multiplicative scaling action \(\sigma_\times\) of \cref{def:gaTranslationP1} (a genuine
2870	  total morphism --- no rational-map extension needed). Apply the additive decomposition
2871	  \cref{lem:hom_additivity_over_product} (Milne Cor~1.5) --- whose Lean signature requires only the
2872	  \emph{first} factor complete --- with \(V = \mathbb P^1\) (proper), \(W = \mathbb G_m\), and base
2873	  points \(0 \in \mathbb P^1\), \(1 \in \mathbb G_m\); here \(h(0, 1) = f(1 \cdot 0) = f(0) = 0_A\), as
2874	  required. The decomposition writes \(h = (p \fatsemi f_0) + (q \fatsemi g)\) with \(f_0\) the
2875	  \(V\)-axis restriction \(x \mapsto h(x, 1) = f(1 \cdot x) = f(x)\) (so \(f_0 = f\)) and \(g\) the
2876	  \(W\)-axis restriction \(\lambda \mapsto h(0, \lambda) = f(\lambda \cdot 0) = f(0) = 0_A\).
2877	  \textbf{The \(W\)-axis collapses (the load-bearing scaling fixed point).} Because \(0\) is a
2878	  \emph{fixed point} of scaling, \(\sigma_\times(0, \lambda) = 0\) for all \(\lambda\)
2879	  (\cref{lem:gmScaling_fixes_zero}), so the \(W\)-axis
2880	  restriction \(g\) is the \emph{constant} morphism at \(0_A\) --- the identity of the hom-group
2881	  \(\mathrm{Hom}(\mathbb G_m, A)\). The corresponding summand \(q \fatsemi g\) is therefore the group
2882	  identity, and the decomposition collapses to
2883	  \[ h = p \fatsemi f, \qquad\text{i.e.}\qquad
2884	     \sigma_\times \fatsemi f = \mathrm{pr}_1 \fatsemi f, \qquad
2885	     \text{``}f(\lambda x) = f(x)\text{''} \quad (x \in \mathbb P^1,\ \lambda \in \mathbb G_m). \]
2886	  \textbf{Constancy via density.} Specialise \(x = 1\) (precompose with $\lambda \mapsto (1,
2887	  \lambda)\(): \)f(\lambda) = f(1)\( for all \)\lambda \in \mathbb G_m\(, i.e.\ \)f|_{\mathbb G_m}$ is
2888	  the constant morphism at \(f(1)\). Since \(\mathbb G_m\) is dense in the irreducible \(\mathbb P^1\)
2889	  and \(A\) is separated, the two morphisms \(f\) and \(\mathrm{const}_{f(1)}\), agreeing on the
2890	  non-empty open \(\mathbb G_m\), agree everywhere by the dominant-source/separated-target rigidity
2891	  handle \cref{thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen} (the same \texttt{ext\_of\_isDominant} family
2892	  \texttt{rigidity\_core} uses). Hence \(f\) is constant, with value \(f(1)\); in the normalised
2893	  picture \(f(0) = 0_A\), so the constant is \(0_A\) (the original \(f(0)\) before translation).
2894	  \medskip
2895	  \noindent\emph{Multi-factor remark.} For the general unirational statement of Proposition~3.10,
2896	  one writes \(\beta(x_1, \ldots, x_d) = \sum_i \beta_i(x_i)\) via the additive decomposition
2897	  (\cref{lem:hom_additivity_over_product}) and applies this base case to each $\beta_i \colon
2898	  \mathbb P^1 \to A\(; we need only the \)d = 1\( case here. The Milne-faithful \)\mathbb
2899	  G_a\(-additive route (\cref{lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial}, via \)\mathrm{Hom}(\mathbb G_a, A) = 0$,
2900	  \cref{lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial}) reaches the same conclusion and is retained as an alternative,
2901	  but is \emph{not} on this critical path.

2956	\section{The headline: rigidity for a pointed genus-\(0\) curve}
2957	\begin{theorem}
2958	\leanok
2959	[Rigidity: a pointed morphism from a genus-\(0\) curve to an abelian variety is constant]
2960	  \label{prop:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV}
2961	  
2962	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme}
2963	  Let \(\bar k\) be an algebraically closed field (\emph{arbitrary characteristic}; no
2964	  \(\mathtt{CharZero}\) hypothesis). Let \(C\) be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve
2965	  over \(\bar k\) with \(\genus(C) = 0\), and let \(A\) be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible
2966	  group scheme over \(\bar k\) (an abelian variety) with identity \(\eta_A\). Then for every
2967	  morphism \(f \colon C \to A\) such that \(p \fatsemi f = \eta_A\) for some \(\bar k\)-point
2968	  \(p \colon \mathbf 1 \to C\), the morphism \(f\) equals the constant morphism at the identity,
2969	  \(f = \mathtt{toUnit}\,C \fatsemi \eta_A\).
2970	  This is the project's committed characteristic-free statement that the genus-\(0\)
2971	  Albanese-witness \(\mathtt{genusZeroWitness}\) consumes. Its signature mirrors
2972	  \(\mathtt{AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity\_over\_kbar}\) of \cref{chap:RigidityKbar}
2973	  \emph{verbatim except} that the \([\mathtt{CharZero}\ \bar k]\) instance is dropped: same
2974	  curve typeclasses (\(\mathtt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}\ 1\), \(\mathtt{IsProper}\),
2975	  \(\mathtt{GeometricallyIrreducible}\) on \(C\)), same abelian-variety typeclasses
2976	  (\(\mathtt{GrpObj}\), \(\mathtt{IsProper}\), \(\mathtt{Smooth}\), \(\mathtt{GeometricallyIrreducible}\)
2977	  on \(A\)), and the same pointed-morphism conclusion shape $f = \mathtt{toUnit}\,C \fatsemi
2978	  \eta[A]$.
2979	\end{theorem}
2980	\begin{proof}
2981	  By \cref{thm:genus_zero_curve_iso_p1} the genus-\(0\) curve \(C\) over the algebraically closed
2982	  field \(\bar k\) is isomorphic to \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) via some isomorphism
2983	  \(\varphi \colon C \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ } \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\). The composite
2984	  \(f \circ \varphi^{-1} \colon \mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to A\) is then a morphism from
2985	  \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) to the abelian variety \(A\), hence constant by
2986	  \cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}: its image is a single \(\bar k\)-point \(a_0 \in A\).
2987	  Therefore \(f\) itself is constant with value \(a_0\). The pointed hypothesis
2988	  \(p \fatsemi f = \eta_A\) evaluates this constant at the \(\bar k\)-point \(p\) and pins it to the
2989	  identity, \(a_0 = \eta_A\). Consequently \(f\) factors through the terminal object as the
2990	  constant morphism at the identity, \(f = \mathtt{toUnit}\,C \fatsemi \eta_A\).
2991	  No differential (\(df = 0\)) input, no \(H^0(C, \Omega_C) = 0\) / Serre-duality decomposition,
2992	  and no Picard representability is used; the argument is valid in arbitrary characteristic.
2993	  Once formalised it lets the \([\mathtt{CharZero}\ \bar k]\) hypothesis carried by
2994	  \(\mathtt{rigidity\_over\_kbar}\) be dropped, and (after the \(\bar k \to k\) descent step hosted
2995	  in \cref{chap:Jacobian}, not here) feeds \(\mathtt{genusZeroWitness}.\mathtt{key}\).

1326	  \(\{v_i^{dv_i}/\prod v_j^{dv_j}\}\) is the whole ring \(\top\). Specialising to
1327	  \(d = 1\), \(\iota' = \mathrm{Fin}\,2\), \(v = (X_0, X_1)\), and exponent vector
1328	  \(dv = (1, 1)\), the relevant generator \(\prod v_i^{dv_i} = X_0 \cdot X_1\) collapses
1329	  along the localisation at \(X_i\) (the \(X_i\)-component is already a unit, so only the
1330	  \(X_{1-i}/X_i\) ratio remains as a non-trivial generator), and the conclusion is that
1331	  \(\mathrm{Algebra.adjoin}\,\mathcal A_0\,\{X_{1-i}/X_i\} = \top\) inside
1332	  \(\mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i\). This is exactly the image of
1333	  \(\mathrm{mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway}_i\), so the map is surjective. \qed
1334	\end{lemma}

1905	[\(\overline{\mathbb P^1}\) is reduced]
1906	  \label{lem:projlinebar_isReduced}
1907	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.projectiveLineBar_isReduced}
1908	  % Archon-original Lean realisation of an elementary textbook fact (projective space over
1909	  % a field is reduced) via the explicit chart cover of \cref{def:projlinebar_affine_cover}
1910	  % and the canonical \(\mathrm{val}\)-injection of a homogeneous localization into the
1911	  % corresponding plain localization. No external % SOURCE QUOTE needed.
1912	  The scheme underlying \(\overline{\mathbb P^1}\) is reduced. By the two-chart affine cover
1913	  (\cref{def:projlinebar_affine_cover}) it suffices to show that each chart ring is
1914	  reduced. The chart-\(i\) ring \(\mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i\) embeds into the ordinary
1915	  localization \(\bar k[X_0, X_1][X_i^{-1}]\) of the polynomial ring \(\bar k[X_0, X_1]\) at
1916	  the powers of \(X_i\) via the canonical injection $\mathrm{val} \colon
1917	  \mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i \hookrightarrow \bar k[X_0, X_1][X_i^{-1}]$
1918	  (\(\mathtt{HomogeneousLocalization.val\_injective}\)); the codomain is a localization of a
1919	  polynomial ring at a non-zero-divisor (the powers of \(X_i\)), hence a domain, hence
1920	  reduced. A subring of a reduced ring is reduced
1921	  (\(\mathtt{Function.Injective.isDomain}\) on the \(\mathrm{val}\)-embedding), so each chart
1922	  ring is a domain --- in particular reduced --- and the global reducedness follows.
1923	\end{lemma}

1926	[The \(\mathbb G_m\)-scaling action \(\sigma_\times \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \to \mathbb P^1\) (primary), and the \(\mathbb G_a\)-translation companion \(\sigma\)]
1927	  \label{def:gaTranslationP1}
1928	  
1929	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gmScalingP1}
1930	  % NOTE (iter-171 refresh of iter-170 decision): the option (c) inline chart-glue
1931	  % commitment LANDED its body skeleton iter-171. `gmScalingP1` now has a CONCRETE
1932	  % body via `Over.homMk ((gmScalingP1_cover).glueMorphisms gmScalingP1_chart
1933	  % gmScalingP1_chart_agreement) gmScalingP1_over_coherence` — no buried sorries.
1934	  % Step A (chart-side ring maps `gmScalingP1_chart{0,1}_ringMap`) + the cover
1935	  % `gmScalingP1_cover` are axiom-clean iter-171. The body's `sorryAx` taint flows
1936	  % through three named top-level internal scaffold sorries:
1937	  % `gmScalingP1_chart` (per-chart scheme morphism), `gmScalingP1_chart_agreement`
1938	  % (cocycle), `gmScalingP1_over_coherence` (over-side coherence). iter-172+ closes
1939	  % these via `pullbackSpecIso ≫ Spec.map _ ≫ Proj.awayι` + `Scheme.Cover.hom_ext`.
1940	  % See `analogies/gmscaling-deep.md` for the full 6-step decomposition.
1941	  % Archon-original Lean encoding; the chart computations below are elementary and characteristic-free.
1942	  % No external % SOURCE QUOTE for the encoding. The mathematical fact -- \(\mathbb G_m\) acts on
1943	  % \(\mathbb P^1\) by scalings fixing \(0\) and \(\infty\), \(\mathbb G_a\) by translations fixing \(\infty\)
1944	  % -- is standard (the Borel subgroup of \(\mathrm{PGL}_2\) acting on \(\mathbb P^1\)).
1945	  % NOTE (iter-164/167): the load-bearing action for the PRIMARY genus-0 route is now the
1946	  % MULTIPLICATIVE scaling action \(\sigma_\times\) (\lean name gmScalingP1, landed iter-165/166;
1947	  % pinned in this block's \lean{...} hook). The \(\mathbb G_a\)-translation \(\sigma\) (\lean name
1948	  % gaTranslationP1 [expected]) is retained only for the demoted additive alternative and is
1949	  % intentionally not yet a Lean target.
1950	  \textbf{The scaling action (primary).} The \emph{multiplicative scaling action}
1951	  \[ \sigma_\times \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \to \mathbb P^1, \qquad
1952	     \text{``}\sigma_\times(x, \lambda) = \lambda x\text{''} \]
1953	  (\lean name \texttt{gmScalingP1}) is a \emph{genuine total} scheme morphism on all of
1954	  \(\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m\) --- no indeterminacy, characteristic-free (M\"obius scaling
1955	  fixing both \(0\) and \(\infty\)) --- defined chartwise:
1956	  \begin{itemize}
1957	    \item On \(\mathbb A^1 \times \mathbb G_m\) (target chart \(x\)): \((x, \lambda) \mapsto \lambda x\),
1958	      a polynomial map, regular everywhere.
1959	    \item Near \(\infty\) (target chart \(u = 1/x\)): the target coordinate \(1/(\lambda x)\) rewrites as
1960	      \(u/\lambda\), regular everywhere on this chart because \(\lambda \in \mathbb G_m\) is invertible.
1961	  \end{itemize}
1962	  The two target charts cover the image and overlap consistently, so \(\sigma_\times\) is defined on
1963	  the whole surface. It is a left action of the group \(\mathbb G_m\) on \(\mathbb P^1\):
1964	  \(\sigma_\times(x, 1) = x\) and $\sigma_\times(\sigma_\times(x, \lambda), \mu) = \sigma_\times(x,
1965	  \lambda\mu)\(. Crucially it has \emph{two fixed points}, \)\sigma_\times(0, \lambda) = 0$ and
1966	  \(\sigma_\times(\infty, \lambda) = \infty\) for all \(\lambda\); the fixed point \(0\) is what makes the
1967	  \(W\)-axis of the additive decomposition collapse in the primary base-case proof
1968	  (\cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}, \cref{rmk:base_case_fourth_route}).
1969	  \medskip
1970	  \noindent\textbf{Companion: the \(\mathbb G_a\)-translation action (demoted route only).} For the
1971	  Milne-faithful additive alternative we also record the \emph{translation action}
1972	  \[ \sigma \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_a \to \mathbb P^1, \qquad
1973	     \text{``}\sigma(x, y) = x + y\text{''} \]
1974	  (\lean name \texttt{gaTranslationP1} [expected]), likewise a \emph{total} scheme morphism on all
1975	  of \(\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_a\), defined chartwise: on \(\mathbb A^1 \times \mathbb G_a\)
1976	  (chart \(x\)) it is the polynomial map \((x, y) \mapsto x + y\); near \(\infty\) (\(u = 1/x\)) the
1977	  coordinate \(1/(x + y)\) rewrites as \(u/(1 + u y)\), regular at \(u = 0\) (value \(0\) there --- \(\sigma\)
1978	  fixes \(\infty\) for every \(y\)), while the locus \(x + y = 0\) is covered by the chart \(x\) on which it
1979	  is the finite value \(0\). It is a left action of \(\mathbb G_a\) on \(\mathbb P^1\) fixing \(\infty\):
1980	  \(\sigma(x, 0) = x\) and \(\sigma(\sigma(x, y), y') = \sigma(x, y + y')\). This companion underlies
1981	  only the alternative proof of \cref{lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial} and the demoted $\mathbb
1982	  G_a$-additive close of the base case (\cref{lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial}); it is \emph{not} on the
1983	  genus-\(0\) critical path.
1984	\end{definition}

2006	  Let \(C\) be a category and let
2007	  \(f_1 \colon W \to S\), \(f_2 \colon X \to S\), \(g_1 \colon Y \to T\),
2008	  \(g_2 \colon Z \to T\), \(i_1 \colon W \to Y\), \(i_2 \colon X \to Z\), \(i_3 \colon S \to T\)

2015	  Mathlib ships \(\mathtt{pullback.lift\_fst}\) as \(\mathtt{@[reassoc]}\) only --- not

2017	  \(\mathtt{pullback.map} \fatsemi \mathtt{pullback.fst}\) is not collapsed by \(\mathtt{simp}\).

2020	  \(\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_cover\_intersection\_X\_iso}\) chain (whose STEP~4 uses
2021	  \(\mathtt{asIso (pullback.map \ldots)}\) for the inner
2022	  \(\mathtt{Proj.pullbackAwayιIso}\) lift). Candidate for upstream Mathlib contribution.

23	\section{Setup and motivation}
24	\label{sec:cross01_substrate_setup}
25	
26	The Lane B cocycle \texttt{gmScalingP1\_chart\_agreement\_cross01} reduces, after
27	the iter-188 (III.c) separated-locus structural setup, to a single substantive
28	factorisation: a pair morphism
29	\(s_{\text{pair}} : T \to T_0 \times_X T_1\) into a fibre product over the
30	projective line bar \(\mathrm{PLB}_{\overline{k}}\) must factor through the
31	diagonal closed immersion
32	\(\Delta : T_0 \times_{X} T_1 \hookrightarrow T_0 \times_{\overline{k}} T_1\)
33	of the separated-over-\(\overline{k}\) target. Concretely the missing primitive is
34	the equivalence
35	\[
36	  \exists\, h : Y \to Z,\ h \circ i = g \iff \mathrm{range}(g) \subseteq \mathrm{range}(i)
37	\]
38	for a closed immersion \(i\) with reduced source/target hypothesis, which Mathlib
39	ships only in the kernel-inequality form (\(\mathtt{IsClosedImmersion.lift}\),
40	keyed on \(f.\mathrm{ker} \le g.\mathrm{ker}\)). Substrate~1 fills this gap.
41	
42	The same Lane B cocycle, together with the two off-target instances
43	\texttt{gm\_geomIrred} and \texttt{projGm\_isReduced}, also requires
44	\[
45	  \mathrm{IsDomain}\bigl(\mathrm{Away}_{\overline{k}}\,f \otimes_{\overline{k}} \mathrm{GmRing}\,\overline{k}\bigr)
46	\]
47	for a homogeneous generator \(f\) of positive degree in the
48	\(\mathrm{projectiveLineBar}\) grading. Substrate~2 supplies this via a
49	canonical iso chain
50	\[
51	  \mathrm{Away}\,f \otimes_{\overline{k}} \overline{k}[t]
52	  \;\cong\; \mathrm{MvPolynomial}_{\mathrm{Unit}}(\mathrm{Away}\,f)
53	\]
54	followed by localisation at \(X()\), reducing the claim to the
55	already-formalised pattern from \texttt{projectiveLineBar\_isReduced}.
56	
57	\section{Closed-immersion lift by range inclusion}
58	\label{sec:lift_iff_range_subset}
59	
60	\begin{theorem}\leanok
61	[lift through closed immersion via range containment]
62	  \label{thm:IsClosedImmersion_lift_iff_range_subset}
63	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.IsClosedImmersion.lift_iff_range_subset}
64	  Let \(X,Y,Z\) be schemes (Type \(u\)), let \(i : Z \to X\) be a closed
65	  immersion with \(Z\) reduced, and let \(g : Y \to X\) be a
66	  quasi-compact morphism with \(Y\) reduced. Then there exists a
67	  morphism \(h : Y \to Z\) with \(h \circ i = g\) if and only if the
68	  topological range of \(g\) is contained in the topological range of
69	  \(i\):
70	  \[
71	    (\exists\, h : Y \to Z,\ h \circ i = g)
72	    \;\iff\;
73	    \mathrm{range}(g_{\text{base}}) \subseteq \mathrm{range}(i_{\text{base}}).
74	  \]
75	\end{theorem}
76	\begin{proof}
77	
78	
79	\leanok
80	  \emph{Forward direction} is range pushdown: if \(g = h \circ i\)
81	  then every point in \(\mathrm{range}(g_{\text{base}})\) is the image
82	  under \(i_{\text{base}}\) of \(h_{\text{base}}(y)\) for the
83	  corresponding preimage \(y\).
84	
85	  \emph{Backward direction} threads the Galois connection between
86	  vanishing-ideal and support of an ideal sheaf. The proof has six
87	  steps; the Mathlib primitives cited are present at the project's
88	  pinned commit (\texttt{b80f227}).
89	
90	  \begin{enumerate}
91	  \item \emph{Range of a closed immersion is its kernel support.}
92	    Because \(i\) is a closed immersion, \(i\) factors (up to
93	    isomorphism) as the canonical subscheme inclusion of its kernel
94	    ideal sheaf, so
95	    \(\mathrm{range}(i_{\text{base}}) = i.\mathrm{ker}.\mathrm{support}\).
96	    (Mathlib:
97	    \texttt{IdealSheafData.range\_subschemeι} +
98	    \texttt{Scheme.Hom.toImage}.)
99	  \item \emph{Range of \(g\) lies in \(g.\mathrm{ker}.\mathrm{support}\).}
100	    The unconditional inclusion
101	    \(\mathrm{range}(g_{\text{base}}) \subseteq g.\mathrm{ker}.\mathrm{support}\)
102	    is shipped (\texttt{Scheme.Hom.range\_subset\_ker\_support}); under
103	    \([\mathrm{QuasiCompact}\;g]\), \(g.\mathrm{ker}.\mathrm{support}\)
104	    equals \(\overline{\mathrm{range}\,g_{\text{base}}}\)
105	    (\texttt{Scheme.Hom.support\_ker}). Since
106	    \(i.\mathrm{ker}.\mathrm{support}\) is closed and contains
107	    \(\mathrm{range}(g)\) by hypothesis, the closure also lies in
108	    \(i.\mathrm{ker}.\mathrm{support}\):
109	    \(g.\mathrm{ker}.\mathrm{support} \subseteq i.\mathrm{ker}.\mathrm{support}\).
110	    (Mathlib: \texttt{closure\_minimal}.)
111	  \item \emph{\(Y\) reduced \(\Rightarrow\) \(g.\mathrm{ker}\) is radical.}
112	    Under \([\mathrm{QuasiCompact}\;g]\),
113	    \(g.\mathrm{ker}.\mathrm{ideal}\,U = \mathrm{ker}(g.\mathrm{app}\,U)\)
114	    (\texttt{Scheme.Hom.ker\_apply}). Reducedness of \(Y\) makes this
115	    ring kernel a radical ideal pointwise on each affine open, so
116	    \(g.\mathrm{ker}.\mathrm{radical} = g.\mathrm{ker}\).
117	  \item \emph{\(Z\) reduced \(\Rightarrow\) \(i.\mathrm{ker}\) is
118	    radical.} Identical argument with \(i\) in place of \(g\)
119	    (closed immersions are quasi-compact, so the
120	    \texttt{ker\_apply} hypothesis is satisfied automatically).
121	  \item \emph{Galois-connection chain.} Combining Steps~2--4 with
122	    \texttt{IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal\_support} (which equates
123	    the vanishing ideal of a support with the radical) gives
124	    \[
125	      i.\mathrm{ker}
126	        = i.\mathrm{ker}.\mathrm{radical}
127	        = \mathrm{vanishingIdeal}(i.\mathrm{ker}.\mathrm{support})
128	        \le \mathrm{vanishingIdeal}(g.\mathrm{ker}.\mathrm{support})
129	        = g.\mathrm{ker}.\mathrm{radical}
130	        = g.\mathrm{ker}.
131	    \]
132	    The middle inequality uses the antitone half of the Galois
133	    connection (\texttt{IdealSheafData.gc}).
134	  \item \emph{Apply Mathlib's kernel-inequality lift.} The proof
135	    concludes by feeding \(\mathrm{hker} : i.\mathrm{ker} \le g.\mathrm{ker}\)
136	    to \texttt{IsClosedImmersion.lift} and reading off its
137	    factorisation lemma \texttt{lift\_fac}.
138	  \end{enumerate}
139	\end{proof}

141	\section{Tensor reducedness over the algebraic closure}
142	\label{sec:tensor_AwayGm_isDomain}
143	
144	\begin{theorem}\leanok
145	[domain structure on \(\mathrm{Away}\,f \otimes_{\overline{k}} \mathrm{GmRing}\)]
146	  \label{thm:gmRing_tensor_homogeneousAway_isDomain}
147	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gmRing_tensor_homogeneousAway_isDomain}
148	  Let \(\overline{k}\) be a field, let \(d \ge 1\), and let \(f\) be a
149	  non-zero homogeneous polynomial of degree \(d\) in the
150	  \(\mathrm{projectiveLineBar}\) grading on
151	  \(\mathrm{MvPolynomial}(\mathrm{Fin}\,2)\;\overline{k}\). Then the
152	  tensor product
153	  \[
154	    \mathrm{HomogeneousLocalization.Away}\bigl(\mathrm{projectiveLineBarGrading}\,\overline{k}\bigr)\,f
155	    \;\otimes_{\overline{k}}\;
156	    \mathrm{GmRing}\;\overline{k}
157	  \]
158	  is a domain (and hence reduced).
159	\end{theorem}
160	\begin{proof}
161	
162	\leanok
163	  The proof exhibits a canonical isomorphism of \(\overline{k}\)-algebras
164	  identifying the tensor product with the localisation of a
165	  polynomial ring over a domain at a non-zero-divisor; both operations
166	  preserve the domain property.
167	
168	  \begin{enumerate}
169	  \item \emph{Domain instance on the homogeneous localisation.}
170	    \(\mathrm{Away}\,f := \mathrm{HomogeneousLocalization.Away}(\mathrm{projectiveLineBarGrading})\,f\)
171	    embeds via the \texttt{val\_injective} bridge into
172	    \(\mathrm{Localization.Away}\,f\); since the polynomial ring
173	    \(\mathrm{MvPolynomial}(\mathrm{Fin}\,2)\;\overline{k}\) is a domain
174	    and \(f \ne 0\), the localisation is a domain, and the bridge
175	    transports the property to \(\mathrm{Away}\,f\). (Already used in
176	    \texttt{projectiveLineBar\_isReduced}; see
177	    \texttt{GmScaling.lean} L734--738 for the precise instance
178	    threading.)
179	  \item \emph{Collapse the tensor with the polynomial factor.}
180	    Recalling
181	    \(\mathrm{GmRing}\;\overline{k}
182	       = \mathrm{Localization.Away}(X()\,:\,\mathrm{MvPolynomial}\;\mathrm{Unit}\;\overline{k})\),
183	    apply \texttt{MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv} to get
184	    \[
185	      \mathrm{Away}\,f \otimes_{\overline{k}} \mathrm{MvPolynomial}\;\mathrm{Unit}\;\overline{k}
186	      \;\cong\;
187	      \mathrm{MvPolynomial}\;\mathrm{Unit}\;(\mathrm{Away}\,f),
188	    \]
189	    a \(\overline{k}\)-algebra (in fact \(\mathrm{Away}\,f\)-algebra)
190	    isomorphism.
191	  \item \emph{Push the localisation through the tensor.}
192	    Tensoring with the localisation step
193	    \(\mathrm{MvPolynomial}\;\mathrm{Unit}\;\overline{k} \to \mathrm{GmRing}\;\overline{k}\)
194	    factors through Mathlib's tensor-base-change lemma
195	    \texttt{IsLocalization.Away.tensorRightEquiv}, yielding an
196	    isomorphism
197	    \[
198	      \mathrm{Away}\,f \otimes_{\overline{k}} \mathrm{GmRing}\;\overline{k}
199	      \;\cong\;
200	      \mathrm{Localization.Away}(\iota(X())\,:\,\mathrm{MvPolynomial}\;\mathrm{Unit}\;(\mathrm{Away}\,f))
201	    \]
202	    where \(\iota\) is the canonical algebra map. The threading uses
203	    \texttt{IsScalarTower} compatibility and the
204	    \texttt{Algebra}/\(\mathrm{IsLocalization.Away}\) instance on the
205	    tensored polynomial ring.
206	  \item \emph{Right-hand side is a domain.}
207	    \(\mathrm{MvPolynomial}\;\mathrm{Unit}\;(\mathrm{Away}\,f)\) is a
208	    polynomial ring over a domain (Step~1), hence a domain by the
209	    standard instance. The generator \(X()\) is non-zero
210	    (\texttt{MvPolynomial.X\_ne\_zero}), so its powers form a
211	    submonoid of the non-zero-divisors
212	    (\texttt{powers\_le\_nonZeroDivisors\_of\_noZeroDivisors}); the
213	    localisation is then a domain by
214	    \texttt{IsLocalization.isDomain\_localization}.
215	  \end{enumerate}
216	
217	  Transferring the domain property along the iso of Step~3 closes the
218	  goal. Reducedness is a direct corollary of being a domain.
219	\end{proof}

1	\chapter{The relative cotangent presheaf}
2	\label{chap:differentials}
3	
4	This chapter develops the relative cotangent \emph{presheaf} \(\Omega_{X/S}\) of a morphism of schemes \(f \colon X \to S\), starting from the ring-theoretic K\"ahler differential module \(\Omega_{B/A}\) already available in Mathlib. The output is a presheaf of \(\struct{X}\)-modules whose sections on each open \(V \subseteq X\) are the K\"ahler differential module of the ring map \((f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S})(V) \to \struct{X}(V)\) induced by \(f\), where \(f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\) denotes the \emph{presheaf-level} inverse-image functor (left Kan extension along \((\mathrm{Opens.map}\,f.\mathrm{base}).\mathrm{op}\)).
5	
6	The downstream role of this chapter is to provide the \emph{forward direction} of the standard smoothness criterion: smoothness of \(f\) implies that on every affine chart \(V \to U\) (with \(V \subseteq X\), \(U \subseteq S\) affine and \(f V \subseteq U\)) the appLE-algebra K\"ahler differential \(\Omega_{\Gamma(X, V)\,/\,\Gamma(S, U)}\) is a free \(\Gamma(X, V)\)-module of rank \(n\). This is the Jacobian-criterion direction and is what connects to the dimension hypothesis on the Jacobian: the smoothness of the curve \(C \to \Spec k\) in the Jacobian definition is the special case \(n = 1\), and the relative dimension of \(\Jac(C)\) is read off from the rank via the same forward implication. The bridge between this affine-chart-wise algebra-K\"ahler form and the section module of the relative cotangent presheaf \(\Omega_{X/S}\) was the former content of milestone~M1; the bridge declaration was excised iter-126 (zero in-tree consumers) and replaced in this chapter by the standalone K\"ahler-localization utilities of Section~REF, the off-loop Mathlib-PR work of which proceeds from \texttt{analogies/relative-differentials-presheaf-bridge.md}. The converse implication of the forward smoothness criterion is \emph{mathematically false} as stated and is documented separately in Section~REF.
7	
8	\section{The relative cotangent presheaf}
9	
10	\begin{definition}
11	
12	\leanok
13	[Relative cotangent presheaf of a morphism]
14	  \label{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
15	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf}
16	  Let \(f \colon X \to S\) be a morphism of schemes. The presheaf-level inverse-image presheaf of rings \(f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S}\) on \(X\) (the left Kan extension of \(\struct{S}\) along \((\mathrm{Opens.map}\,f.\mathrm{base}).\mathrm{op}\), given on each open \(V \subseteq X\) by the colimit \(\mathrm{colim}_{W : \,f V \subseteq W \subseteq S}\,\struct{S}(W)\)) is paired with \(\struct{X}\) by the canonical adjunction-transpose \(f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S} \to \struct{X}\) of the comorphism \(f^{\sharp}\colon\struct{S}\to f_{*}\struct{X}\). Applying the presheaf-of-modules K\"ahler differential construction \(\mathrm{relativeDifferentials}'\) to this map of presheaves of commutative rings produces a presheaf of \(\struct{X}\)-modules on \(X\), the \emph{relative cotangent presheaf} \(\Omega_{X/S}\).
17	\end{definition}
18	
19	\begin{lemma}
20	
21	\leanok
22	[Sections of \(\Omega_{X/S}\) on an open]
23	  \label{lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj}
24	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_obj_kaehler}
25	  
26	  For every open \(V \subseteq X\), the sections of the relative cotangent presheaf are, by construction, the K\"ahler differential module of the ring map \((f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S})(V) \to \struct{X}(V)\) induced by \(f\):
27	  \[
28	    \Omega_{X/S}(V) \;=\; \Omega_{\struct{X}(V) \,/\, (f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S})(V)}.
29	  \]
30	  Here \((f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S})(V) = \mathrm{colim}_{W : \,f V \subseteq W \subseteq S}\,\struct{S}(W)\) is the presheaf-level inverse-image section ring, \emph{not} \(\struct{S}(U)\) for an arbitrary affine open \(U \supseteq f V\). The relationship between this colimit-source K\"ahler module and the classical ring-theoretic \(\Omega_{B/A}\) for \(A = \struct{S}(U)\), \(B = \struct{X}(V)\) on an affine chart \(V = \Spec B \subseteq f^{-1}U\) with \(U = \Spec A \subseteq S\) affine is the content of the bridge documented in Section~REF.
31	\end{lemma}
32	
33	\begin{proof}
34	  
35	  \leanok
36	  By definition of \(\mathrm{relativeDifferentials}'\): the construction is built object-wise to be the Mathlib \texttt{KaehlerDifferential} module of the ring map at each open. The identification is therefore by \texttt{rfl} after unfolding the definition. The colimit description of \((f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S})(V)\) is the definition of \texttt{TopCat.Presheaf.pullback} as the left Kan extension along \((\mathrm{Opens.map}\,f.\mathrm{base}).\mathrm{op}\) in Mathlib.
37	\end{proof}
38	
39	\begin{remark}[Comparison with Mathlib's ring-level \(\Omega_{B/A}\)]
40	  \label{rem:kahler_compatibility}
41	  Mathlib provides the ring-theoretic K\"ahler differential module \(\Omega_{B/A}\) together with the universal derivation \(\mathrm{d} \colon B \to \Omega_{B/A}\). The construction of \(\Omega_{X/S}\) on each open is, by definition, this Mathlib module applied at the ring level of the presheaf, so all ring-level Mathlib lemmas about \(\Omega_{B/A}\) transfer verbatim under Lemma~REF — but for the \emph{colimit-source} ring \((f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S})(V)\), not for \(\struct{S}(U)\). Transferring to the classical \(A = \struct{S}(U)\) source on an affine chart is the bridge of Section~REF.
42	\end{remark}

44	\section{Smoothness criterion for \(\Omega_{X/S}\) (forward direction)}
45	
46	The forward direction of the textbook smoothness criterion identifies a necessary condition on the relative K\"ahler differentials: every point of \(X\) admits an affine chart \(V \to U\) over which the appLE-algebra K\"ahler module \(\Omega_{B/A}\) is free of the prescribed rank, with \(A = \struct{S}(U)\) and \(B = \struct{X}(V)\). This is the global version of the algebraic-side fact that an \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}~\(n\)~algebra has a free K\"ahler differential module of rank \(n\). We state and prove the theorem directly on the appLE-algebra K\"ahler module, sidestepping the presheaf bridge of Section~REF. The converse implication (a local-freeness hypothesis on \(\Omega\) alone implying smoothness) is \emph{false}; see Section~REF.
47	
48	\begin{theorem}\leanok
49	[Smooth implies \(\Omega\) locally free (algebra-K\"ahler form)]
50	  \label{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega}
51	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega}
52	  
53	  Let \(f \colon X \to S\) be a morphism of schemes and let \(n \in \mathbb{N}\). If \(f\) is smooth of relative dimension \(n\), then for every point \(x \in X\) there exist an affine open \(U \subseteq S\), an affine open \(V \subseteq X\) with \(x \in V\) and \(V \subseteq f^{-1}(U)\), such that the K\"ahler differential module
54	  \[
55	    \Omega_{\Gamma(X, V) \,/\, \Gamma(S, U)}
56	  \]
57	  (over the appLE algebra structure \(\Gamma(S, U) \to \Gamma(X, V)\) induced by \(f\)) is a free \(\Gamma(X, V)\)-module of rank \(n\).
58	\end{theorem}
59	
60	\begin{proof}
61	  
62	  \leanok
63	  Fix \(x \in X\). We use the abbreviation \(A := \Gamma(S, U)\) and \(B := \Gamma(X, V)\) for the section rings on the affine chart we will produce; the algebra map \(A \to B\) is the appLE map \((f.\mathrm{appLE}\,U\,V\,e).\mathrm{hom}\) induced by \(f\).
64	
65	  \emph{Step 1: extract a standard-smooth chart at \(x\).} The auto-generated \texttt{mk\_iff} bridge
66	  \[
67	    \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.smoothOfRelativeDimension\_iff} \quad [\text{verified}]
68	  \]
69	  (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Smooth}; produced by the \texttt{@[mk\_iff]} attribute on the class \texttt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}) unfolds the hypothesis \texttt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}~\(n\)~\(f\) into: for every \(x \in X\), there exist affine opens \(U \subseteq S\) and \(V \subseteq X\) with \(x \in V\) and \(V \leq f^{-1}U\), such that the induced algebra map \((f.\mathrm{appLE}\,U\,V\,e).\mathrm{hom}\colon A \to B\) satisfies \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}~\(n\)~\(A\)~\(B\).
70	
71	  \emph{Step 2: pass to plain standard-smooth.} The lemma
72	  \[
73	    \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.isStandardSmooth} \quad [\text{verified}]
74	  \]
75	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmooth}) yields \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth}~\(A\)~\(B\) from the relative-dimension-tagged form, unlocking the cotangent-module machinery in the next two steps.
76	
77	  \emph{Step 3: free K\"ahler module on the chart.} The Mathlib instance
78	  \[
79	    \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free\_kaehlerDifferential} \quad [\text{verified}]
80	  \]
81	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmoothCotangent}) synthesises \texttt{Module.Free}~\(B\)~\(\Omega_{B/A}\) from \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth}~\(A\)~\(B\).
82	
83	  \emph{Step 4: pin the rank.} The theorem
84	  \[
85	    \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank\_kaehlerDifferential} \quad [\text{verified}]
86	  \]
87	  (same file as Step 3) gives \(\rank_B\,\Omega_{B/A} = n\) under the hypotheses \texttt{Nontrivial}~\(B\) and \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}~\(n\)~\(A\)~\(B\).
88	
89	  \emph{Step 4.5: discharge the \texttt{Nontrivial}~\(B\) side condition.} The chart witness \(x \in V\) shows that the open \(V \subseteq X\) is non-empty as a set, so its structure-sheaf section ring \(B = \Gamma(X, V) = \struct{X}(V)\) is non-zero by \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.component\_nontrivial} (the structure sheaf of a scheme has \texttt{Nontrivial} sections on a non-empty open). This supplies the \texttt{Nontrivial}~\(B\) hypothesis required by Step 4.
90	
91	  Combining Steps 1--4.5, the chart \((U, V, e)\) produced by Step 1 satisfies \(x \in V\), \(\mathrm{IsAffineOpen}\,U\), \(\mathrm{IsAffineOpen}\,V\), and under the appLE algebra structure \(A = \Gamma(S, U) \to B = \Gamma(X, V)\), the K\"ahler module \(\Omega_{B/A}\) is a free \(B\)-module of rank \(n\). As \(x \in X\) was arbitrary, the statement follows.
92	
93	  This is the forward direction of the Jacobian criterion: Stacks Tag~02G1 (and Hartshorne~II.8, Theorem~II.5.2 in the Noetherian case).
94	
95	  \emph{Mathlib name summary.} The five \texttt{[verified]} closure pieces the proof relies on are
96	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.smoothOfRelativeDimension\_iff} (Step 1),
97	  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.isStandardSmooth} (Step 2),
98	  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free\_kaehlerDifferential} (Step 3),
99	  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank\_kaehlerDifferential} (Step 4),
100	  and \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.component\_nontrivial} (Step 4.5).
101	\end{proof}
102	
103	\begin{remark}[Lean naming]
104	  \label{rem:smooth_class_naming}
105	  The Lean class for ``smooth of relative dimension \(n\)'' is \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.SmoothOfRelativeDimension}; the spelling \texttt{IsSmoothOfRelativeDimension} that appeared in earlier iterations of this project is a deprecated alias in Mathlib \texttt{b80f227}. The \texttt{\textbackslash lean\{...\}} hint above names the non-deprecated form.
106	\end{remark}

109	\label{sec:bridge}

111	The presheaf-form \(\Omega_{X/S}\) of Definition~REF and the algebra-K\"ahler form \(\Omega_{B / A}\) on an affine chart of Theorem~REF are connected, on an affine chart, by a canonical \(B\)-linear isomorphism. Earlier iterations of the project (iter-121 through iter-125) hosted this isomorphism in the Lean tree as a \emph{bridge} declaration \texttt{relativeDifferentialsPresheaf\_equiv\_kaehler\_appLE} backed by a Mathlib gap-fill sub-lemma \texttt{appLE\_isLocalization} (the load-bearing localization-at-the-image-submonoid claim, packaged as a multi-LOC residual sorry whose closure was concrete but had no in-tree consumer). The forward-direction smoothness criterion (Theorem~REF) closed iter-120 in algebra-K\"ahler form \emph{independently} of the bridge, so the bridge was project-local infrastructure with zero downstream consumer.
112	
113	\textbf{Excise decision (iter-126).} Per the iter-126 strategy-critic CHALLENGE, ratified by the iter-126 user hint endorsing "shortest path qualified by your role is to fill mathlib gaps", the bridge declaration and its load-bearing sub-lemma were excised from the Lean tree iter-126: closing the bridge produced neither a downstream consumer nor a Mathlib PR (the extractable PR candidate is M1.d — Lemma~REF below — and is \emph{independent} of the bridge). The two retained declarations in this section are the standalone K\"ahler-localization utilities that survive as upstream-PR-quality material; off-loop PR work proceeds from \texttt{analogies/relative-differentials-presheaf-bridge.md}.

115	\begin{lemma}\leanok
116	[K\"ahler module of a localization is trivial]
117	  \label{lem:kaehler_localization_subsingleton}
118	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.kaehler_localization_subsingleton}
119	  Let \(A \to L\) be a localization at a multiplicative subset \(M \subseteq A\). Then \(\Omega_{L / A}\) is subsingleton (the zero \(L\)-module).
120	\end{lemma}
121	
122	\begin{proof}
123	
124	\leanok
125	  This is a thin re-export of Mathlib's existing infrastructure: \texttt{Algebra.FormallyUnramified.of\_isLocalization}~\texttt{[verified]} (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Unramified.Basic}, line 303) produces the instance \texttt{Algebra.FormallyUnramified}~\(A\)~\(L\) from any \texttt{IsLocalization}~\(M\)~\(L\) predicate; the instance \texttt{Algebra.FormallyUnramified.subsingleton\_kaehlerDifferential}~\texttt{[verified]} (same file, lines 57--59) then yields \texttt{Subsingleton}~\((\mathrm{KaehlerDifferential}~A~L)\). Hence the project-side lemma is a two-line re-export (\(\mathtt{haveI} := \mathtt{Algebra.FormallyUnramified.of\_isLocalization}~M\) \texttt{; infer\_instance}); contra an earlier framing, no Mathlib gap-fill is required for this lemma.
126	\end{proof}
127	
128	\begin{lemma}\leanok
129	[Tower-cancellation equivalence for K\"ahler modules under localization]
130	  \label{lem:kaehler_quotient_localization_iso}
131	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.kaehler_quotient_localization_iso}
132	  
133	  Let \(A \to L\) be a localization (in the sense of \texttt{IsLocalization}), and let \(L \to B\) be a ring map forming a scalar tower \(A \to L \to B\). Then the canonical \(B\)-linear map \(\Omega_{B/A} \to \Omega_{B/L}\) induced by the tower is a \(B\)-linear equivalence.
134	\end{lemma}
135	
136	\begin{proof}
137	  
138	  \leanok
139	  The second fundamental exact sequence of K\"ahler differentials reads
140	  \[
141	    B \otimes_{L} \Omega_{L/A} \;\longrightarrow\; \Omega_{B/A} \;\twoheadrightarrow\; \Omega_{B/L} \;\longrightarrow\; 0.
142	  \]
143	  By Lemma~REF, the left term vanishes (since \(\Omega_{L/A}\) is subsingleton and tensoring against it gives the zero module). Therefore the right surjection has zero kernel and is a \(B\)-linear equivalence. The Mathlib closure pieces are \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.exact\_mapBaseChange\_map}~\texttt{[verified]} (the exact sequence above) and \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.map\_surjective}~\texttt{[verified]} (the right-hand surjection), both in \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Kaehler.Basic}; the project-side declaration packages these into a \texttt{LinearEquiv} via \texttt{LinearEquiv.ofBijective}. This is the most extractable Mathlib contribution candidate of milestone~M1: a clean generalisation of \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.tensorKaehlerEquivOfFormallyEtale} to the ``only the lower step is formally unramified'' case, candidate name \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.equivOfFormallyUnramified}.
144	\end{proof}

151	\section{Converse direction (milestone M4)}
152	\label{sec:converse-out-of-scope}
153	
154	This direction is recorded as an optional future milestone~M4 (see \texttt{STRATEGY.md}). Closure requires the deformation-theoretic hypothesis \texttt{Subsingleton (Algebra.H1Cotangent}~\(A\)~\(B\)\texttt{)}, which the project may wire up if a downstream consumer demands the iff form. The remainder of this section documents the obstruction in concrete form: a counterexample showing that without that hypothesis the converse fails, and a comparison with the Mathlib converse lemma at the ring level.
155	
156	The reverse implication
157	\[
158	  \forall x\in X,\ \exists\,U\text{ affine},\ \Omega_{X/S}(U)\text{ free of rank }n
159	  \quad\Longrightarrow\quad f \text{ smooth of relative dimension } n
160	\]
161	is \emph{mathematically false} as stated, even under the auxiliary hypothesis that \(f\) is locally of finite presentation. Local freeness of \(\Omega\) alone does not imply flatness, and flatness (equivalently, formal smoothness via vanishing of the first Andr\'e--Quillen cohomology) is genuine deformation-theoretic content not encoded in the section-module hypothesis. This is why the iff form of the criterion present in earlier iterations was demoted to a forward implication.
162	
163	\begin{remark}[Explicit counterexample]
164	  \label{rem:converse_counterexample}
165	  Let \(k\) be a field and consider the morphism \(f \colon \Spec k \to \Spec k[t]\) induced by the surjection \(k[t] \twoheadrightarrow k\) sending \(t \mapsto 0\). Then \(f\) is locally of finite presentation, its source \(\Spec k\) has a unique affine open (itself), and the K\"ahler differential module \(\Omega_{k/k[t]} = 0\) is free of rank \(0\) (the trivial \texttt{Module.Basis} indexed by the empty set witnesses this). Yet \(f\) is the closed immersion of a non-open point of \(\Spec k[t]\), hence is not flat and therefore not smooth, so \texttt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}~\(0\)~\(f\) fails. The local-freeness hypothesis on \(\Omega\) is therefore strictly weaker than smoothness, and the converse implication of the iff form fails on this very example.
166	\end{remark}
167	
168	\begin{remark}[The Mathlib converse lemma and its hypotheses]
169	  \label{rem:converse_lemma_hypotheses}
170	  Mathlib does provide a converse-direction lemma at the ring level,
171	  \[
172	    \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.of\_basis\_kaehlerDifferential} \quad [\text{verified}]
173	  \]
174	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmoothOfFree}). Its three hypotheses make the missing content explicit:
175	  \begin{itemize}
176	    \item \texttt{Algebra.FinitePresentation}~\(A\)~\(B\);
177	    \item \texttt{Subsingleton (Algebra.H1Cotangent}~\(A\)~\(B\)\texttt{)} --- vanishing of the first Andr\'e--Quillen cohomology, i.e.\ formal smoothness;
178	    \item a basis \(b \colon \texttt{Module.Basis}~I~B~\Omega_{B/A}\) whose range lies in the image of the universal derivation, \(\mathrm{Set.range}\,(\texttt{Algebra.KaehlerDifferential.D}~A~B)\).
179	  \end{itemize}
180	  None of these three is supplied by the section-module hypothesis of the would-be iff. The first-cotangent-vanishing hypothesis in particular is the deformation-theoretic input that distinguishes ``\(\Omega\) locally free'' from ``smooth''; without it, the counterexample of Remark~REF would already break the converse. Establishing \texttt{Subsingleton (Algebra.H1Cotangent}~\(A\)~\(B\)\texttt{)} from project-side hypotheses (e.g.\ via a flatness assumption added to the input) is the work that would close the converse, and is the content of milestone~M4.
181	\end{remark}
182	
183	\begin{remark}[Cross-reference to Stacks 02G1]
184	  \label{rem:stacks_02G1}
185	  The standard textbook biconditional ``\(f\) is smooth of relative dimension \(d\) iff \(f\) is flat, locally of finite presentation, and \(\Omega_{X/S}\) is locally free of rank \(d\)'' is Stacks Tag~02G1 (and is the Noetherian-case Hartshorne~II.8, Theorem~II.5.2). The flatness conjunct is precisely what is missing from a hypothesis that only asserts local freeness of \(\Omega\); supplying it amounts to the deformation-theoretic content discussed in Remark~REF. The forward implication proved as Theorem~REF above corresponds to the easy direction of the same tag.
186	\end{remark}

188	\section{Content scheduled for later milestones}
189	
190	Each bullet below is a future milestone whose Mathlib infrastructure is not yet available in the pinned snapshot \texttt{b80f227}; inclusion in this list does \emph{not} mean the project has abandoned it. The roadmap progression is: M1 (this iter) \(\to\) M2 / M3 (existence routes) \(\to\) M4 (converse, Section~REF) \(\to\) M5+ (sheafification, cotangent exact sequence, etc., listed here). Each item lists the deferred concept, the missing Mathlib piece, and one cross-reference for forward-looking reinstatement.
191	
192	\begin{itemize}
193	  \item \textbf{Sheaf condition for \(\Omega_{X/S}\) (M5).} The relative cotangent presheaf is, on an affine basis, locally isomorphic to Mathlib's quasi-coherent sheaf \(\widetilde{\Omega_{B/A}}\) on each affine chart, and the unique-gluing characterisation of the sheaf property holds basis-wise. No off-the-shelf Mathlib lemma packages ``\texttt{Scheme.PresheafOfModules}-sheaf-on-affine-basis \(\Rightarrow\) sheaf on \(X\)'' however; the cofinality descent against \texttt{TopCat.Presheaf.isSheaf\_iff\_isSheafOpensLeCover} is the deferred work for this milestone. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~II.5 (sheaves from affine charts) and Stacks Tag~009H.
194	
195	  \item \textbf{Cotangent exact sequence (M6).} For a composition of morphisms \(X \to Y \to S\), there is an exact sequence of quasi-coherent \(\struct{X}\)-modules
196	  \[
197	    f^{*}\Omega_{Y/S} \;\xrightarrow{\;\alpha\;}\; \Omega_{X/S} \;\xrightarrow{\;\beta\;}\;
198	      \Omega_{X/Y} \;\longrightarrow\; 0.
199	  \]
200	  The ring-level ingredients (\texttt{KaehlerDifferential.exact\_mapBaseChange\_map} and the surjectivity of the right map) are in Mathlib, but the exactness conjunct requires \texttt{SheafOfModules.exact\_iff\_stalkwise} or an equivalent section-wise+sheafification route through homology-preserving instances of the forget functor; both are Mathlib gaps. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~II.8, Proposition~8.4A and Stacks Tag~01UV.
201	
202	  \item \textbf{Cotangent space at a section (M7).} A corollary of the smoothness criterion above, pulled back along a section \(s \colon S \to X\): if \(f\) is smooth of relative dimension \(n\) and \(s\) is a section, then \(s^{*}\Omega_{X/S}\) is a locally free \(\struct{S}\)-module of rank \(n\). The natural Lean framing uses \texttt{Scheme.Modules.pullback} against the sheaf-bundled \(\mathrm{relativeDifferentials}\), which was trimmed in iter-117; a presheaf-form analogue against \texttt{PresheafOfModules.pullback} would need a separate formulation. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~II.8, Corollary~8.10.
203	
204	  \item \textbf{Serre-duality genus identity (M8).} On a smooth proper geometrically integral curve \(C/k\),
205	  \[
206	    \dim_{k} H^{0}(C, \Omega_{C/k}) \;=\; \dim_{k} H^{1}(C, \struct{C}),
207	  \]
208	  both sides being equal to the genus \(g(C)\). Mathlib \texttt{b80f227} has no dualizing sheaf, no trace morphism for proper morphisms, and no Zariski coherent cohomology of \(\struct{X}\)-modules, so the equality cannot yet be expressed within the project's Mathlib snapshot. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~III.7 and Stacks Tag~0FVU.
209	\end{itemize}
